steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to present the methods used in the 2020 study — i.e., what the researchers did, not what they found.

steps.title: Look for a methods-only sentence in the notes
steps.content: Method facts in the notes: placed nest boxes, tagged 138 female wood ducks with RFID trackers, recorded how many nest boxes each duck visited. Results facts (to avoid): 48.5% visited one box, 13.0% visited 10+, younger ducks visited more. The correct choice should use only the method facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• "By recording how many nest boxes each duck visited, the researchers discovered that only a relatively small percentage (13.0%) of the ducks visited 10 or more nest boxes.": reports a finding, not methods. ✗
  • "After tracking how many nest boxes the 138 wood ducks visited, the researchers found that the younger ducks tended to visit more nest boxes than the older ducks.": also a finding, not methodology. ✗
  • "The researchers tagged 138 female wood ducks with radio frequency ID trackers and recorded how many nest boxes each duck visited during the nesting season.": pure methodology, drawn directly from the notes. ✓
  • "The researchers investigated each nesting site for signs that it had been visited by the female wood ducks.": fabricated — the notes describe RFID tracking of ducks, not site inspections. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
